bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues are valueD for everything, giving me utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. There's no offer history to read, so I'll open strong with my ideal bid.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my top bid. I'll maintain my maximum utility bid since there's no reason to concede yet - the opponent is clearly starting from their ideal position just as I am.

The opponent is consistently offering all valueA (their best, my worst), showing no concession at all. We're very early (t=0.04), so there's no need to concede yet. I'll maintain my top-utility bid (all valueD) since it's still very early in the negotiation and the opponent hasn't moved at all. I'll stay firm to signal that I won't capitulate easily.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.06) and both sides are holding firm at their maximum utility bids. The opponent is consistently offering all valueA (which gives me minimum utility of 0.2 across all issues), while I've been offering all valueD. Since it's still very early, I should stay near my top utility but make a small concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on issueA (my lowest weight issue at 0.1) by dropping to valueC, while holding firm on the more important issues.

I'll keep issueD, issueC, and issueB at valueD since those are my highest-weight issues.

This signals willingness to negotiate while protecting my high-weight issues.
